subject: civic
source_title: First Term SS3 Civic Education E-Learning Note
learning_objectives: State the features of constitutional democracy

sections.heading: Definition
sections.content: Constitutional democracy is a government of the people, by the people and for the people which is based on the supremacy of the constitution. It can also be explained as a democratic system in which the will of the people prevail, their fundamental human rights recognized and protected, and the principle of rule of law strictly adhered to.

sections.heading: Types of Constitutional Democracy
sections.content: DIRECT DEMOCRACY: This is also known as classical democracy. It is a form of democracy in which all adult citizens take active part in the management and political running of the state. INDIRECT DEMOCRACY: This is also called representative democracy. This form of democracy is built on the principle of majority rule because it allows a few elected representatives to make decisions and formulate policies on behalf of the people.

sections.heading: Features of Constitutional Democracy
sections.content: POPULAR SOVEREIGNTY, MAJORITY RULE, MINORITY RIGHT, RULE OF LAW, PERIODIC ELECTION, EQUAL ACCESS TO POLICAL OPPORTUNITIES, INDEPENDENT JUDICIARY.
